description: Calculates a dynamic shade of a color by adjusting its lightness in LCH color space.
The base color to shade.
The target lightness value to move toward.
The percentage to move toward the target lightness.
The offset added or subtracted based on direction.
The dynamically shaded color in LCH color space
Digests a theme schema and returns a resolved theme map.
A theme schema.
A theme map with resolved values.
Get the resolved theme for a schema
code: border-radius: (rem(5px), rem(0), rem(8px)) // default, min and max radius

description: Resolves schema values, where the keys of the passed value map are the names
of the functions to be called and the value for a given key is the argument
the function should be called with.
The map to be used as instruction set.
The resolved value.
Resolve primary 500 color
Retrieves the CSS custom property reference for the given key in the component theme.
The source theme to be used to read values from.
A key from the theme map to assign as value to the property.
A value to be used if the CSS property is not defined.
The CSS reference of the property in the theme.
Assign the color property in a set of rules to a value from the theme.
Returns a value between an upper and lower bound.
The preferred value.
The minimum value.
The maximum allowed value.
The clamped value
Returns true if a key-value pair from a theme map should be emitted as a CSS variable.
Filters out map values (nested sub-themes) and the special 'prefix' key.
The theme property key.
The theme property value.
Returns true if a value contains a sizable() expression that depends on
component-scoped variables (--is-large, --is-medium, --is-small).
These expressions cannot be resolved at global scope because CSS custom
properties resolve at the declaration scope, not the consumption scope.
The theme property value to check.
Collects all emittable keys from a cleaned theme map.
Used to build the list of local variable names that need to be
rewritten to global token references.
A theme map with ignored keys already removed.
A list of emittable key names.
Rewrites local CSS variable references in a value to global token references.
For example, `var(--background)` becomes `var(--ig-avatar-background)`.
The original theme property value.
The component name (e.g., 'avatar').
The list of emittable keys whose local refs should be rewritten.
The value with all local var references rewritten to global token names.
